sections.paragraph: ἅπαξ λεγόμ. i.q. הָמָה, הָמַל, הָמַר to make a noise, to rage. Inf. Eze 5:7, יַעַן הֲמָנְכֶם מִן־הַגֹּויִם “because that ye raged yet more (against God) than the nations (which are around).” Compare הָמָה Psa 2:1 46:7 . It may also be that הָמַן is a secondary root, formed from הָמֹון. The Hebrew interpreters regard הֲמָנְכֶם as being for הֲמֹנְכֶם, and that this is the verbal noun הָמֹון itself. [So Ges. in Thes. and Corr.]
